course_title: Management of Adult Voice and Fluency Disorders
course_description:
This course provides students with theoretical knowledge and clinical skills in the assessment and management of voice and fluency disorders in adults. Students will develop a comprehensive understanding of normal voice production, vocal pathology that may disrupt vocal function, and evidence-based approaches for diagnosis and intervention of voice disorders in adults. The course also explores the nature, aetiology, and characteristics of fluency disorders in adults, with an emphasis on differential diagnosis and evidence-based treatment approaches.

Through a combination of lectures, case studies, and practical tutorials, students will gain competencies in:

· Conducting comprehensive voice and fluency assessments

· Analysing normal and disordered voice characteristics and fluency behaviours

· Applying evidence-based treatment techniques to a range of voice and fluency disorders

· Understanding the psychosocial impacts of voice and fluency disorders on clients and their families

By the end of this course, students will possess the professional knowledge and clinical skills necessary to effectively assess and treat adults with voice and fluency disorders.

timetable:

UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with foundation knowledge relating to voice and fluency disorders in adults and to develop professional competencies in the assessment, diagnosis and clinical management of these disorders. [SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.3-1.6, 2.5, 2.6, 3.2-3.5]

learning_outcomes.description: Describe and explain the theoretical foundations of normal and disordered voice and fluency in adults. (SPA PS Standards 1.1, 1.2)
Identify and apply assessment tools and procedures to collect and interpret data relevant to adult voice and fluency disorders. (SPA PS Standards 1.5, 3.1, 3.2)

Apply clinical reasoning skills to the interpretation of assessment data to formulate appropriate short-term treatment goals and intervention plans for adults with voice and fluency disorders. (SPA PS Standards 1.4, 1.6, 1.7, 3.3, 3.4)
Describe and apply principles of person- and family-centred care in the delivery of voice and fluency management, including the importance of shared decision-making. (SPA PS Standards 1.4, 3.4, 3.5)

Recognise the role of cultural and linguistic diversity in the assessment and management of adult voice and fluency disorders, including the needs of gender-diverse individuals. (SPA PS Standards 1.6, 3.5)
Demonstrate professional conduct and communication consistent with the Speech Pathology Australia Professional Standards during simulated or classroom-based activities. (SPA PS Standards 1.3, 4.1)

assessment_summary.title: Reflection task: Voice and fluency disorders management and knowledge transfer

assessment_details.title: In-semester exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This in-semester exam will assess knowledge and skills acquired from weekly pre-recorded lectures and tutorials in relation to the assessment, diagnosis, and management of voice and fluency disorders (Weeks 1-5). This assessment will encompass short answer questions including case-based questions relating to assessment interpretation, differential diagnosis and management options for voice and fluency disorders. Students will also be required to perform an online assessment of stuttering behaviours and vocal features from a video recording during this exam. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Reflection task: Voice and fluency disorders management and knowledge transfer
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be required to submit a 4-5 minute video reflection demonstrating their ability to transfer knowledge and skills from voice disorder management to fluency disorder management in adults. In this reflection, students will analyse how the principles and skills used in voice disorders management can be applied to fluency disorders, while acknowledging the unique aspects of each condition. Students should consider the common ground between these areas, including assessment, diagnosis, therapy approaches, and interdisciplinary collaboration, and demonstrate how their knowledge and skills in voice disorders provide a foundation for addressing fluency disorders. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This assessment task is to be submitted via the course eLearning Blackboard site.
Submitting assessment in Blackboard and Turnitin
When assessment is submitted through Blackboard you will receive a confirmation page as a digital receipt.ﾠThis information will also be sent to your student
email account.ﾠPlease ensure you keep this email.
When assessment is submitted through Turnitin you will receive a confirmation page displaying “Submission Complete!” and can then proceed to download your
digital receiptﾠfrom yourﾠAssignment inbox.ﾠIt is suggested that you save a copy of the receipt for your personal records.
Instructions on how to submit assignments through Blackboard and Turnitin are available at:
https://elearning.uq.edu.au/student-guides-ultra/turnitin-gradescope-ultra-students/submit-your-turnitin-assignment-ultra-student
https://elearning.uq.edu.au/student-guides-ultra/blackboard-assignments-ultra-student/assignment-submission-instructions-ultra-student?p=1#1
Please review this information so you understand how to successfully submit your assessment items.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for an extension request via my.UQ > my.UQ Dashboard > my. Requests and provide evidence of circumstance, as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than the assessment item submission date. Further information regarding what constitutes an acceptable reason for an extension is listed on the my.UQ website: Applying for an extension - my.UQ - University of Queensland.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Late submission of assessment items will attract a penalty of 10% of the mark per day late or, for work graded on a 1-7 scale, a deduction of one grade per day
(including weekends and public holidays), unless a formal extension has been organised prior to the due date and time.
Requests for extension of assessment due date must be made online, via your my.UQ portal prior to the submission date and time. Late applications may not be
accepted. The request must also contain documentation satisfactorily supporting the request. If an extension is approved, you will be notified via your my.UQ
portal and the new date for submission provided.

assessment_details.title: Voice/Fluency Simulation Clinic
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The Voice/ Fluency Simulation Clinic is a MUST PASS assessment in SPCH3114. The simulation clinic is made up of two (2) sessions. The first clinic will provide an opportunity to synthesise case information, provide patient education and practise intervention techniques in relation to two (2) cases (i.e, one (1) case with a fluency disorder and one (1) case with voice disorder), via a formative assessment of your skills (i.e., performance feedback is provided but does not contribute to overall grade). The second simulation clinic will provide a summative assessment of your performance in relation to the same tasks from simulation clinic 1 in relation to two (2) new cases. In preparation for the simulation clinic, a tutorial will be conducted in Week 8. Students will attend the voice and fluency simulation clinic sessions in Week 10 and Week 12. For each simulation session students will be assessed individually. Tutorials in Week 8 are designed to assist students with preparation and practise opportunities for this clinic. 1. Simulation Clinic 1 – Week 10 (Formative) Students will be assessed individually. Feedback will be provided from Practice Educators and simulated patients following the sessions. 2. Simulation Clinic 2 – Week 12 (Summative) Students will be assessed individually under examination conditions. In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: In-person lectures (Weeks 1 and 13) Two in-person lectures will be delivered in this course. An introduction to the course and related assessment will be provided in Week 1, as well as foundational content in relation to adult voice and fluency disorders. In Week 13, a wrap up lecture will be provided, which will include preparation for the end of semester exam. Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 9
learning_activities.topic: Online self-directed learning Pre-recorded lecture content will be available on Blackboard across weeks 2-9. It is expected that students will engage with this content prior to attending the weekly tutorials. Up to four (4) hours of pre-recorded lecture material (voice and fluency content combined) will be provided on the course Blackboard site each week. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 11
learning_activities.topic: Weekly on-campus tutorials Weekly tutorials (2 x 2 hour tutorials) will be held in weeks 2-5, 7-9, and 11 of semester. Tutorials will be practically-based and will facilitate student understanding and application of the course content. Week 8 and 11 tutorials will focus on simulation preparation and feedback following the formative sessions. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 12 (04 May - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic: Voice/ fluency simulation clinic In weeks 10 and 12 of semester, students will engage in a voice/ fluency simulation clinic. This simulation clinic provides students with scaffolded, experiential learning opportunities to develop and demonstrate clinical reasoning and intervention skills with voice and fluency disorders in adulthood. Formative Simulation Clinic (Week 10) In this low-stakes learning environment, students will work individually with simulated patients. One (1) case will present with a fluency disorder and one (1) case will present with a voice disorder. This session allows students to: Apply clinical reasoning to synthesise case information Practice patient education and counselling skills Implement evidence-based intervention techniques Receive constructive feedback from Practice Educators and simulated patients to refine your clinical approach The formative nature means students can take risks, learn from mistakes, and develop their skills without grade consequences. Summative Simulation Clinic (Week 12) Building on the formative experience and feedback, students will demonstrate mastery of the same clinical tasks with two (2) new cases (one fluency, one voice disorder). This individual pass/fail (MUST PASS) assessment under examination conditions will showcase your ability to independently manage adult cases with voice and fluency disorders. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L06
